curationNotes: Matt Pocock's public media presence is strongest in two distinct phases: his conference-talk era (2021-2023) as a TypeScript educator at React Finland, React Advanced, and React Day Berlin, all catalogued on GitNation; and his AI engineering era (2025-2026) centered on aihero.dev, where he writes directly about the grill-me skill, the skills CLI, and engineering/productivity workflows. The skills repository itself (github.com/mattpocock/skills, 132k stars, 11.5k forks) is the primary anchor — it is first-party, high-reach, and directly indexes the suite, grill-me, engineering, and productivity skills. No confirmed Syntax.fm or JS Party guest appearance was found through direct URL verification (only name-drop mentions in three JS Party episodes); a dedicated podcast episode featuring Matt as the main guest remains an open gap.

evidence.magnitudeRaw: GitHub repository with 132k stars and 11.5k forks (verified on page); documents the full skills suite, grill-me, engineering, and productivity skills directly
evidence.notes: Primary first-party source for the suite. Stars verified from README page fetch. Includes /grill-me, /grill-with-docs, /tdd, /diagnose, /improve-codebase-architecture, /to-prd, /to-issues, /caveman, /handoff. Newsletter cited as ~60k subscribers.

evidence.magnitudeRaw: Blog post titled 'My Grill Me Skill Went Viral' on aihero.dev, updated March 23 2026; direct authorial account of viral adoption
evidence.notes: Matt documents the grill-me skill's viral spread. Describes it as his most flexible skill, used for 45-minute structured AI interviews before implementation. Self-authored, first-party evidence.

evidence.magnitudeRaw: Article '5 Agent Skills I Use Every Day' on aihero.dev (updated March 16 2026) — covers grill-me, /to-prd, /to-issues, /tdd, /improve-codebase-architecture directly
evidence.notes: Covers exactly the skills in mattpocock/skills suite. First-party editorial by the skill author. Describes engineering and productivity workflow patterns in depth.

evidence.magnitudeRaw: Article '9 Things People Get Wrong With /grill-me and /grill-with-docs' cross-posted on totaltypescript.com, updated May 25 2026
evidence.notes: Published on totaltypescript.com (the flagship 40k+ developer platform) — significantly increases reach. Covers grill-me and grill-with-docs skills directly. Confirms cross-platform editorial investment in the skills framework.

evidence.magnitudeRaw: Skills changelog article on aihero.dev (April 30 2026) documenting /grill-with-docs release, /diagnose, /triage; 46.6k stars cited at time of writing
evidence.notes: Announces major skills reorganisation into Engineering/Productivity/Misc/Deprecated/Personal categories. Confirms ongoing active development and editorial voice around the suite.

evidence.magnitudeRaw: Article 'How To Make Codebases AI Agents Love' on aihero.dev — directly addresses the engineering workflow philosophy behind the engineering skill
evidence.notes: Covers the deep modules and architectural philosophy that underpins the /improve-codebase-architecture and broader engineering skills. No explicit publication date extracted, inferred 2026.

evidence.magnitudeRaw: Article 'Real-world feature build with Claude Code: every step explained' on aihero.dev, published March 20 2026 — demonstrates grill-me skill in live workflow
evidence.notes: Shows a real project using the grill-me skill as the first step in a Claude Code workflow. Demonstrates practical adoption of the skills suite methodology.

evidence.magnitudeRaw: React Day Berlin 2023 talk 'React's Most Useful Types' — 21 minutes; Matt Pocock as headlining TypeScript educator at major international React conference
evidence.notes: Confirms Matt's status as a prominent conference speaker in the TypeScript/React ecosystem. Establishes authority of the suite creator. GitNation is the primary record; exact YouTube URL not resolved. Date approximate (React Day Berlin 2023).

evidence.magnitudeRaw: React Advanced 2022 talk 'TypeScript and React: Secrets of a Happy Marriage' — 21 minutes on October 24 2022; recorded and indexed on GitNation
evidence.notes: Conference talk at React Advanced London 2022. Verifies Matt's standing as a TypeScript educator at significant international conferences predating the skills CLI.

evidence.magnitudeRaw: React Finland 2021 talk 'Make Legacy Code Delightful with Statecharts' — 22 minutes; published August 31 2021
evidence.notes: Earlier foundational conference appearance. Establishes long-standing engineering credibility underpinning the skills suite. Context predates Total TypeScript.

evidence.magnitudeRaw: JS Party #326 (June 6 2024, changelog.com) — Matt Pocock cited by Eric Clemmons as a leading TypeScript expert in a build-step debate episode
evidence.notes: Not a guest appearance; mentioned as an authority on TypeScript configuration and performance. His TSConfig library also cited. Demonstrates peer recognition among JS ecosystem voices.

evidence.magnitudeRaw: JS Party #348 (Nov 29 2024, changelog.com) — Theo Browne identifies Matt Pocock as the archetypal TypeScript enthusiast at React Summit New York
evidence.notes: Not a guest appearance. Theo's phrasing ('I'm not the TypeScript enthusiast that a Matt Pocock is') confirms Matt's widespread reputation as the leading TypeScript educator in the community.

evidence.magnitudeRaw: Total TypeScript platform — 40k+ developers in community; flagship TypeScript education brand. Skills positioned as companion productivity/engineering workflows for the same audience.
evidence.notes: Total TypeScript is the well-known framework/course that establishes Matt's authority and audience base. The skills suite (mattpocock/skills) is marketed to the same community. This serves as proxy-containment: the suite is implicitly endorsed by the credibility of the TotalTypeScript platform.

gapsIdentified: No confirmed Syntax.fm guest appearance found — Matt Pocock does not appear as a named guest in any episode checked (episodes 237, 464, 487, 604, 700, 813, 849, 851, 941, 1003). A dedicated Syntax Supper Club episode with Matt would be high-value evidence.
No confirmed JS Party guest appearance found — Matt mentioned in 3 episodes but never as an invited guest. A dedicated episode would significantly boost the suite's interview evidence tier.
devtools.fm archive confirms no Matt Pocock episode exists as of June 2026.
No TSConf, JSNation, TypeScript Congress, or ViteConf 2024-2026 session found for Matt Pocock through available sources. His conference activity post-2023 may have shifted to online-first content.

YouTube channel (@mattpocockuk) content could not be scraped directly — YouTube truncates channel pages. Individual video URLs for Total TypeScript tips and AI Hero walkthroughs would add interview-class evidence if view counts are verifiable.

No independent media coverage (TechCrunch, Wired, InfoQ, ThoughtWorks Radar) found mentioning the skills CLI specifically — the framework's adoption is documented primarily through Matt's own channels.
aihero.dev/ai-hero course page returned 404 — the AI Hero course (successor to Total TypeScript) could not be independently verified for enrollment figures or media coverage.
React Day Berlin 2023 YouTube video URL not directly resolved — only GitNation transcription page found. Direct YouTube URL and view count would strengthen the interview evidence.
